title: The Judgment Framework — Q2: Can I verify this without the AI?

sampled_assertions.claim: AI cannot verify its own output; same model self-assessment only
Stock price prediction as unverifiable example
Q1 x Q2 decision matrix (4-row table)
Legal pattern — lawyer checks AI clause against legislation
Asking AI again is not verification; consistency is not correctness

undefined_terms.note: All non-everyday terms defined at first use: 'independent verification' (line 11, bolded with plain definition), 'production question'/'verification question' (table at lines 27-30 with plain meanings), four method names each followed by immediate explanation. 'Anchoring' at line 41 is back-referenced to topic 9.4 — contextual, not cold.

abstract_first.note: Rumour analogy precedes abstraction in Key Concepts. Verification methods each lead with a concrete example. Easy/hard/impossible categories use examples before generalisations.
